description: Remaining carbon budgets from IPCC AR6 Working Group 1 (2021) and Working Group 3 (2022). Budgets represent the cumulative net CO2 emissions from a given start year that would limit global warming to specified temperature thresholds with stated probability levels.
source: IPCC AR6 WG1 Table SPM.2 (2021), AR6 WG3 (2022), updated with observed emissions through 2024
notes: Original AR6 WG1 budgets are from January 2020. These have been adjusted for estimated cumulative emissions 2020-2024 (~200 GtCO2). Non-CO2 warming contribution assumptions follow AR6 central estimates. Budgets are for CO2 only; non-CO2 forcing assumed to follow SSP1-1.9/SSP1-2.6 pathways.

carbonBudgets.name: 1.5°C (50% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: This budget gives a 50% chance of limiting warming to 1.5°C. Extremely tight. Requires immediate, deep cuts. At 2024 emission rates, budget exhausted by ~2033.

carbonBudgets.name: 1.5°C (67% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: Budget for 67% chance of 1.5°C. Effectively already exhausted or very close. This is the most ambitious budget from AR6. Would require halving emissions by 2030.

carbonBudgets.name: 1.7°C (50% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: Intermediate temperature target between 1.5°C and 2°C. Consistent with SSP1-2.6 pathway.

carbonBudgets.name: 1.7°C (67% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: 67% chance of staying below 1.7°C. Consistent with 'well below 2°C' Paris goal.

carbonBudgets.name: 2°C (50% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: 50% chance of limiting to 2°C. More achievable but still requires significant policy action.

carbonBudgets.name: 2°C (67% probability)
carbonBudgets.notes: 67% chance of limiting to 2°C. Core Paris Agreement target. Budget ~960 GtCO2 from 2025. At current rates, exhausted by ~2050.

uncertaintyFactors.description: Permafrost thawing, Amazon dieback, and other carbon cycle feedbacks may release additional CO2/CH4.
Budget size depends on assumed non-CO2 GHG trajectory. Stronger methane reductions expand CO2 budget.
Reducing air pollution (SO2) removes aerosol cooling effect, accelerating warming.
Different datasets for historical warming (1850-1900 baseline) affect remaining budget calculation.
